intent: process the first sample with scanpy

blocks.text: I'll process the first sample (Patient 145, Day 0) with scanpy following the standard QC → normalization → HVG → PCA → clustering workflow.

blocks.content: {"status": "ok", "name": "scrna-qc-clustering", "body": "scRNA-seq QC+clustering (scanpy): read 10x; calculate_qc_metrics; filter n_genes>=200 & pct_mito<0.20; normalize_total(1e4)+log1p; highly_variable_genes; pca; neighbors; umap; leiden; rank_genes_groups. Plots: HVG plot and PCA variance side-by-side; UMAP colored by leiden; use sc.pl.* not hand-rolled matplotlib."}

blocks.text: Perfect! Now let me present a plan based on this recipe:

input.title: Process Patient 145 Day 0 PBMC sample with scanpy
input.summary: QC filtering, normalization, HVG identification, dimensionality reduction (PCA/UMAP), and Leiden clustering on the first COVID-19 PBMC sample
input.rationale: Standard scanpy pipeline to get initial cell-type insights and cluster structure
input.assumptions:
- Processing one sample in isolation (batch effects not a concern for single sample)
  - QC thresholds: ≥200 genes/cell, <20% mitochondrial reads (standard for PBMCs)
  - Leiden clustering for cell-type discovery
  - Homo sapiens, so mitochondrial genes are MT-*

steps.title: Load sample and calculate QC metrics
steps.description: Load GSM5746259 (Day 0) from the registered dataset, compute n_genes, n_counts, percent_mt
expected_outputs: QC metrics calculated; initial data shape confirmed

steps.title: QC filtering
steps.description: Filter cells: n_genes ≥ 200, pct_mt < 20%; report cells/genes retained

steps.title: UMAP and Leiden clustering
steps.description: umap() → leiden(resolution=1.0); plot UMAP colored by leiden cluster
expected_outputs: umap_plot (UMAP by cluster); leiden in obs

steps.title: Marker genes per cluster
steps.description: rank_genes_groups(method='wilcoxon'); summarize top 10 markers per cluster
